title: Peloton Q3 Earnings Preview: 1 Key Metric You'll Want to Know
text: Peloton Q3 2021 Earnings Preview. Key Metric to Watch: Connected fitness subscriptions. The article identifies the number of connected fitness subscriptions as the critical indicator investors should monitor when Peloton reports Q3 results on May 6, 2021. The company reported 1.67 million subscriptions in Q2 and guided to 1.98 million for Q3. Why It Matters: Connected fitness subscriptions reveal the company's earning potential over the long run because they indicate market penetration and growth. As subscription numbers increase, Peloton can spread its fixed content creation costs across more users, improving profitability. Wall Street Expectations: Analysts forecast Q3 revenue of $1.1 billion with a loss per share of $0.12. Market Context: Peloton stock had declined over 36% percent year-to-date as investors worried that gym reopenings and rising vaccination rates would reduce demand for home fitness equipment. However, the author argues these concerns are overstated, noting the company achieved at least 100% annual revenue growth for six consecutive years even before the pandemic.
